cat_sub23b_sub1_sub17.description: Services for interacting with database platforms designed for rapid application development with minimal coding, such as NocoDB, Supabase, and Firebase.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub17.boundary: Not for traditional database management systems requiring direct SQL or programmatic access. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub17.decision_rule: If the service provides access to a database platform primarily marketed for low-code or no-code application development, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub19.name: Specialized Application Database Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub19.description: Services for databases tightly integrated with or built for a specific non-database application domain, like CAD (Revit) or CRM (Dataverse).
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub19.boundary: Not for general-purpose databases. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub19.decision_rule: If the service provides database access that is a component of a larger, specialized software application (e.g., CAD, CRM, B2B SaaS), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub2.name: Vector Database Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub2.description: Services for creating, querying, and managing vector databases used for AI embeddings, semantic search, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub2.boundary: Not for traditional relational databases, NoSQL document stores, or data warehouses. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables vector storage, embedding, or semantic search (e.g., Qdrant, vector search), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub20.name: Agent Memory & Context Persistence Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub20.description: Services that use databases specifically to store, search, and manage the memory, context, or state of AI agents and assistants across sessions.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub20.boundary: Not for databases used for general application data. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub20.decision_rule: If the service's primary purpose is to provide persistent memory, context, or state storage specifically for AI agents/assistants, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub3.name: NoSQL Document & Key-Value Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub3.description: Services for interacting with NoSQL databases that store data as documents, key-value pairs, or wide-column stores, such as MongoDB, Redis, and DynamoDB.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub3.boundary: Not for relational SQL databases, vector databases, or graph databases. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily connects to a NoSQL database for document, key-value, or wide-column storage (e.g., MongoDB, Redis, Firestore), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub4.name: Cloud Data Warehouse Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub4.description: Services for querying and managing large-scale, cloud-native analytical data warehouses like Google BigQuery, Snowflake, and AWS Redshift.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub4.boundary: Not for operational relational databases, vector databases, or real-time OLTP systems. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs analytical queries on a cloud-based data warehouse (e.g., BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub5.name: Real-time Analytics & OLAP Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub5.description: Services for high-speed analytical processing on real-time data streams using databases like Apache Druid, Pinot, ClickHouse, and Trino.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub5.boundary: Not for traditional transactional databases (OLTP) or cloud data warehouses. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables low-latency analytical queries on real-time or streaming data (e.g., ClickHouse, Druid, Trino), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub6.name: Graph Database Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub6.description: Services for interacting with databases designed to store and query data as nodes and relationships, such as Neo4j, Memgraph, and FalkorDB.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub6.boundary: Not for relational, vector, or document databases. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables the creation or querying of graph-based data structures and relationships, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub7.name: Time-Series Database Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub7.description: Services for collecting, querying, and analyzing time-stamped data from databases like InfluxDB, IoTDB, and GreptimeDB.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub7.boundary: Not for general-purpose relational databases or real-time analytics engines. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub7.decision_rule: If the service is specifically designed for storing and querying time-series or sequential metric data, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub8.name: Distributed SQL Database Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub8.description: Services for interacting with globally distributed, scalable SQL databases that offer strong consistency, such as CockroachDB, YugabyteDB, and TiDB.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub8.boundary: Not for single-node relational databases or traditional NoSQL systems. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily connects to a horizontally scalable, distributed SQL database with global consistency guarantees, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub1_sub9.name: Search Engine & Index Services
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub9.description: Services for configuring, managing, and querying dedicated search indices and engines like Algolia, OpenSearch, and Elasticsearch.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub9.boundary: Not for general-purpose databases or vector search databases. See sibling categories for those.
cat_sub23b_sub1_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages a dedicated full-text search index or search engine, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub1.name: Application Performance Monitoring
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub1.description: Services focused on monitoring the performance, health, and behavior of software applications, including traces, errors, and user interactions.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub1.boundary: Does not include infrastructure metrics (see Infrastructure Monitoring), raw logs (see Log Management), or network telemetry (see Network Monitoring).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors the runtime behavior, errors, or performance of a software application or its components, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub10.name: Service Discovery & Configuration
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub10.description: Services for discovering, registering, and managing the configuration and health of services in a distributed system.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub10.boundary: Does not include deep performance monitoring of those services (see Application Performance Monitoring) or their infrastructure (see Infrastructure Monitoring).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides service registry, health checking, or dynamic configuration management (e.g., key-value stores for config), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub11.name: Dashboard & Visualization
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub11.description: Services focused on searching, managing, or interacting with dashboards and visualizations built from observability data.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub11.boundary: Does not include the collection or storage of the underlying data (see other categories). Focus is on the presentation layer.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables searching, retrieving, or managing pre-built dashboards, charts, or visualizations, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub13.name: MLOps & AI Pipeline Monitoring
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub13.description: Services for monitoring, managing, and analyzing machine learning and large language model training pipelines, experiments, and deployments.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub13.boundary: Does not include monitoring of generic applications (see Application Performance Monitoring) or data pipelines (see Data Platform & Pipeline Monitoring). Specific to ML/AI workflows.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily tracks experiments, manages models, or monitors the lifecycle of machine learning or LLM training/deployment pipelines, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub15.name: End-User & Digital Experience Monitoring
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub15.description: Services focused on monitoring the performance, availability, and experience of services from an end-user or external perspective.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub15.boundary: Does not include internal application metrics or infrastructure health. Focus is on external, synthetic, or real-user monitoring.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub15.decision_rule: If the service primarily checks service availability or performance from an external vantage point or monitors real-user interactions, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub16.name: Unified Observability Query
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub16.description: Services that provide a unified interface (often natural language) to query across multiple types of observability data (logs, metrics, traces).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub16.boundary: Does not specialize in a single data type (see Log Management, Distributed Tracing, etc.). Defined by its cross-data-type query capability.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub16.decision_rule: If the service's primary function is to provide a single query interface that spans logs, metrics, and/or traces from various sources, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub17.name: Cloud Platform & Service Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub17.description: Services for monitoring, managing, and interacting with specific cloud platform services (e.g., OpenStack, Kubernetes services, cloud gateways).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub17.boundary: Does not include generic infrastructure monitoring or cost management. Focus is on the control plane and APIs of a specific cloud or platform stack.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub17.decision_rule: If the service primarily interacts with the management APIs of a specific cloud platform (IaaS/PaaS) or its core services (e.g., storage, networking, orchestration), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub18.name: Agent & Automation Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub18.description: Services for monitoring, controlling, and managing the lifecycle of monitoring agents, automation scripts, or long-running processes.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub18.boundary: Does not include monitoring the targets of those agents. Focus is on the health and control of the monitoring/automation components themselves.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors, controls, or manages the agents, daemons, or automation processes responsible for collecting data or performing actions, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub19.name: Observability Platform Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub19.description: Services for managing the configuration, resources, and metadata of the observability platforms themselves (e.g., saved searches, data sources, users).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub19.boundary: Does not include querying the data stored within the platform (see other categories). Focus is on administrative and configuration tasks for the platform.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub19.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages the configuration, saved objects, data sources, or users of an observability platform (like Splunk, Grafana), classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub2.name: Infrastructure Monitoring
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub2.description: Services for monitoring the health, performance, and metrics of underlying compute, storage, and server infrastructure (e.g., VMs, containers, hosts).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub2.boundary: Does not include application-level traces/errors (see Application Performance Monitoring), network connectivity data (see Network Monitoring), or cloud billing data (see Cloud Cost Management).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors metrics and health of servers, containers, virtual machines, or other compute/storage resources, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub20.name: Developer Environment Integration
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub20.description: Services that integrate live observability data (logs, traces, metrics) into local development or testing environments to aid debugging.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub20.boundary: Does not include production monitoring or management. Defined by its purpose of bridging the gap between production data and development workflows.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub20.decision_rule: If the service's primary purpose is to stream or replicate production observability data into a local development environment for debugging purposes, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub3.name: Log Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub3.description: Services dedicated to the collection, storage, search, and analysis of log data from various systems and applications.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub3.boundary: Does not include structured application performance traces (see Application Performance Monitoring), infrastructure metrics (see Infrastructure Monitoring), or cost data (see Cloud Cost Management).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables querying, searching, or analyzing raw log files and event streams, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub4.name: Distributed Tracing
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub4.description: Services focused on collecting, querying, and analyzing distributed traces to understand request flows across microservices or distributed systems.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub4.boundary: Does not include application error/health monitoring (see Application Performance Monitoring), log file analysis (see Log Management), or infrastructure metrics (see Infrastructure Monitoring).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles the ingestion, storage, or querying of end-to-end request traces across service boundaries, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub6.name: Cloud Cost Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub6.description: Services focused on analyzing, reporting, and optimizing spending and resource utilization across cloud service providers.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub6.boundary: Does not include performance or health metrics (see other monitoring categories) or log/trace data (see Log Management / Distributed Tracing).
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily analyzes, reports on, or provides recommendations for cloud infrastructure spending and cost optimization, classify here.

cat_sub23b_sub3_sub7.name: Deployment & Release Management
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub7.description: Services for monitoring and managing the status, health, and logs of software deployments, releases, and pipelines.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub7.boundary: Does not include monitoring of running applications (see Application Performance Monitoring) or underlying infrastructure (see Infrastructure Monitoring). Focus is on the deployment process itself.
cat_sub23b_sub3_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily tracks the status, logs, or health of software deployments, releases, or CI/CD pipelines, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub1.name: Web & Document Data Extraction
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub1.description: Services focused on scraping, crawling, and extracting structured or unstructured data from websites, web pages, and documents.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub1.boundary: Does not include general data transformation, database querying, or workflow orchestration. For database interaction, see 'Database & Warehouse Interaction'. For orchestration, see 'Pipeline & Workflow Orchestration'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily extracts, scrapes, or crawls data from web pages, documents (like PDFs, CSVs, Excel), or the internet, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub11.name: Financial & Market Data
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub11.description: Services that provide, process, or analyze financial instruments, market data, investment research, or blockchain data.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub11.boundary: Does not include general data warehousing, business intelligence for other domains, or customer data. For warehousing, see 'Database & Warehouse Interaction'. For customer data, see 'Customer & Profile Data'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles data related to stocks, crypto, forex, blockchain, or investment research, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub12.name: AI & Model Lifecycle Data
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub12.description: Services that handle data specific to the AI/ML lifecycle, including model evaluation, experiment tracking, prompt management, and vector data.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub12.boundary: Does not include general data processing, code analysis, or business intelligence. For code analysis, see 'Code & Development Data Analysis'. For general processing, see 'Data Transformation & Processing'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages data for AI model training, evaluation, experimentation, or handles vector embeddings for retrieval, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub13.name: Content & Knowledge Management
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub13.description: Services that organize, search, and manage unstructured content, documents, and knowledge into structured graphs or searchable systems.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub13.boundary: Does not include simple web scraping, database interaction, or AI model data. For scraping, see 'Web & Document Data Extraction'. For AI data, see 'AI & Model Lifecycle Data'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily structures, links, and enables semantic search over collections of documents, notes, or knowledge, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub16.name: API Lifecycle & Management
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub16.description: Services that manage the governance, security, usage, and lifecycle of APIs as data products and integration points.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub16.boundary: Does not include data integration via APIs, API testing, or general observability. For integration, see 'Data Integration & Ingestion'. For observability, see 'Observability & Monitoring'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages API tokens, users, analytics, versioning, and deployment as managed assets, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub18.name: Graph Data Management & Analysis
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub18.description: Services specialized in storing, querying, and analyzing data structured as nodes and edges (graphs), including knowledge graphs.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub18.boundary: Does not include relational database interaction, vector databases for AI, or general content management. For relational DBs, see 'Database & Warehouse Interaction'. For vector DBs, see 'AI & Model Lifecycle Data'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily operates on data using graph models (nodes, edges, properties) for traversal, relationship analysis, or knowledge graph management, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub19.name: Schema & Data Format Management
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub19.description: Services focused on defining, generating, converting, and enforcing data schemas, structures, and serialization formats.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub19.boundary: Does not include general data transformation, data quality validation, or data ingestion. For transformation, see 'Data Transformation & Processing'. For quality, see 'Data Quality & Validation'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub19.decision_rule: If the service primarily generates JSON Schemas, converts data between formats (e.g., CSV to JSON), or manages type definitions for data, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub2.name: Database & Warehouse Interaction
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub2.description: Services enabling querying, updating, and managing data within specific databases, data warehouses, or lakehouses.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub2.boundary: Does not include data extraction from the web, workflow orchestration, or data validation/quality. For web data, see 'Web & Document Data Extraction'. For quality, see 'Data Quality & Validation'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides an interface to directly query, manipulate, or manage data within a specific database or data warehouse system (e.g., Snowflake, ClickHouse, Apache Doris), classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub3.name: Pipeline & Workflow Orchestration
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub3.description: Services for building, scheduling, monitoring, and managing data pipelines and multi-step data processing workflows.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub3.boundary: Does not include the specific data transformation logic within a step, nor direct database querying. For transformation tools, see 'Data Transformation & Processing'. For database tools, see 'Database & Warehouse Interaction'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily orchestrates the execution order, dependencies, and lifecycle of data processing steps or pipelines, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub4.name: Data Integration & Ingestion
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub4.description: Services focused on collecting and consolidating data from multiple, disparate sources into a central system or platform.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub4.boundary: Does not include the transformation of the data after ingestion, nor the orchestration of the ingestion process. For transformation, see 'Data Transformation & Processing'. For orchestration, see 'Pipeline & Workflow Orchestration'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily pulls or streams data from various applications, APIs, or sources into a unified location, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub5.name: Data Transformation & Processing
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub5.description: Services that perform operations to clean, reshape, aggregate, or otherwise modify the structure and content of data.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub5.boundary: Does not include data extraction from sources, data storage/querying, or pipeline orchestration. For extraction, see 'Web & Document Data Extraction' or 'Data Integration & Ingestion'. For orchestration, see 'Pipeline & Workflow Orchestration'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily applies logic to convert, clean, aggregate, or compute new values from existing data, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub6.name: Data Quality & Validation
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub6.description: Services that assess, monitor, and enforce the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of data against defined rules or expectations.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub6.boundary: Does not include data transformation, profiling for discovery, or general data processing. For transformation, see 'Data Transformation & Processing'. For discovery, see 'Data Discovery & Cataloging'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily validates data against quality rules, checks for anomalies, or measures data reliability, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub7.name: Data Discovery & Cataloging
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub7.description: Services that inventory, document, and provide searchable access to data assets, including their metadata, lineage, and definitions.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub7.boundary: Does not include data quality checks, data processing, or direct data querying. For quality, see 'Data Quality & Validation'. For querying, see 'Database & Warehouse Interaction'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily helps users find, understand, and track the provenance and relationships of data assets, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub18_sub8.name: Observability & Monitoring
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub8.description: Services for tracking the performance, health, logs, and events of data systems, applications, and infrastructure.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub8.boundary: Does not include data quality validation, pipeline orchestration UI, or business intelligence. For quality, see 'Data Quality & Validation'. For orchestration, see 'Pipeline & Workflow Orchestration'.
cat_sub24a_sub18_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily collects, queries, and analyzes logs, metrics, traces, or events for system health and performance, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub1.name: Business Intelligence & Analytics
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub1.description: Services focused on business intelligence, dashboard creation, and enterprise analytics for decision-making.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub1.boundary: Not for raw database queries without BI context, specialized domain analytics (finance, IoT), or data governance.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables business reporting, dashboarding, or general enterprise data analysis for decision support, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub13.name: Real-time Analytics & OLAP
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub13.description: Services optimized for low-latency, real-time analytical queries on large datasets (OLAP).
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub13.boundary: Not for transactional (OLTP) operations, batch analytics, or simple database queries.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub13.decision_rule: If the service is specifically optimized for real-time, low-latency analytical processing (OLAP) on large datasets, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub14.name: Database Administration & Performance
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub14.description: Services focused on database management, performance tuning, cluster operations, and administrative tasks.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub14.boundary: Not for querying business data, data analysis, or data exploration by end-users.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub14.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs database administration, performance tuning, cluster management, or schema inspection, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub18.name: Multi-Cloud Data Warehouse Query
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub18.description: Services that provide a unified interface to query across multiple major cloud data warehouse platforms.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub18.boundary: Not for services tied to a single database vendor, platform, or non-warehouse data source.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables querying data across multiple, different cloud data warehouse platforms (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks), classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub19.name: Workflow & Project Management Data
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub19.description: Services for querying and managing data from workflow, project management, and collaboration platforms.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub19.boundary: Not for spreadsheets, ERP data, or general business intelligence analytics.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub19.decision_rule: If the service primarily accesses data from workflow, project management, or team collaboration platforms (e.g., Fibery, Airtable as a workflow tool), classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub20.name: General-Purpose Database Interaction
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub20.description: Services for direct SQL querying, exploration, and interaction with general-purpose relational or operational databases.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub20.boundary: Not for domain-specific analytics (finance, IoT), metadata management, or specialized data platforms (vector, graph).
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub20.decision_rule: If the service provides direct, general-purpose querying and interaction with common relational or operational databases without a specific domain focus, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub4.name: IoT & Sensor Data Analytics
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub4.description: Services for managing and analyzing data from Internet of Things devices, sensors, and time-series monitoring systems.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub4.boundary: Not for financial time-series, business logs, or general operational databases.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles data from physical sensors, IoT devices, or specialized time-series monitoring, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub5.name: Graph Data & Relationships
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub5.description: Services for querying and analyzing graph databases, focusing on relationships, networks, and knowledge graphs.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub5.boundary: Not for relational (SQL) business data, time-series data, or vector-based similarity search.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily operates on graph databases or analyzes data as nodes and relationships (networks, knowledge graphs), classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub6.name: Vector & Semantic Search
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub6.description: Services for vector database operations, semantic similarity search, and embedding-based retrieval.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub6.boundary: Not for keyword search in documents, traditional graph relationships, or standard SQL querying.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs similarity search, vector embeddings retrieval, or semantic matching in a vector space, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub7.name: Spreadsheet & Table Management
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub7.description: Services for interacting with spreadsheet data (e.g., Excel, Google Sheets) and multi-dimensional tables.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub7.boundary: Not for database servers, data warehouses, or analytics platforms that are not spreadsheet-based.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily reads, writes, or manages data in spreadsheet formats or multi-dimensional table interfaces, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub8.name: Metadata Management & Catalog
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub8.description: Services for discovering, cataloging, and governing data assets, including lineage and data quality.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub8.boundary: Not for querying the actual business data, performing analytics, or managing spreadsheet content.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily searches data catalogs, traces data lineage, or manages metadata about data assets, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub19_sub9.name: Code & Computational Notebooks
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub9.description: Services for executing code, interacting with computational notebooks (e.g., Jupyter), and programmatic data analysis.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub9.boundary: Not for direct database querying without code execution, or for managed BI/analytics platforms.
cat_sub24a_sub19_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily executes code (Python, MATLAB, SQL) in notebooks or scripts for data analysis, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub10.name: Knowledge & Information Management
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub10.description: Creating, organizing, retrieving structured knowledge and conducting automated research.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub10.boundary: Focuses on human knowledge organization and automated research. Excludes vector infrastructure, agent memory, and dataset discovery.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub10.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily helps create/manage knowledge or performs automated research and report generation.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub11.name: Agent Systems & Context
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub11.description: Managing memory, context, and testing for AI agents and RAG applications.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub11.boundary: Focuses on agent-level functionality. Excludes vector infrastructure, knowledge management for humans, and model evaluation.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub11.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily manages memory/context for agents or tests/debugs agent/RAG applications.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub13.name: Time Series & Forecasting
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub13.description: Analyzing sequential data over time to make predictions about future trends and events.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub13.boundary: Focuses on temporal patterns and predictions. Excludes real-time event processing and historical business data analysis.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub13.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily analyzes time-series data to make forecasts or predictions.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub14.name: Dataset & Model Discovery
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub14.description: Browsing, searching, and analyzing datasets and machine learning models from repositories.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub14.boundary: Focuses on discovery of external data/model resources. Excludes querying business databases and managing experiments.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub14.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily helps discover, explore, and understand datasets or models from repositories.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub15.name: Automated Data Science Workflows
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub15.description: Automating end-to-end data science pipelines from preprocessing to model selection.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub15.boundary: Focuses on multi-step pipeline automation. Excludes managing individual experiments and executing code snippets.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub15.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily automates multi-step data analysis or machine learning pipelines.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub16.name: API & Model Serving
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub16.description: Providing API endpoints for language models and other AI services with utility functionality.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub16.boundary: Focuses on model serving and API provision. Excludes model management, evaluation, and code execution.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub16.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily provides API endpoints for AI models or utility AI services.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub2.name: Public & Domain-Specific Data
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub2.description: Accessing and analyzing government, blockchain, sports, and other specialized domain datasets.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub2.boundary: Covers structured data from specific public or niche domains. Excludes private business data, general time series, and conversational data.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub2.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily provides access to or analyzes government, blockchain, sports, or other specialized public domain datasets.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub3.name: Vector Search & Embedding Infrastructure
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub3.description: Core services for vector operations, semantic search, embedding generation, and RAG infrastructure.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub3.boundary: Foundational infrastructure layer. Excludes higher-level applications built on top (knowledge management, agent memory).
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub3.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily provides vector search, embedding, or RAG infrastructure as a platform.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub4.name: Model & Experiment Management
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub4.description: Managing ML model lifecycles: searching, running, tracking, and comparing experiments.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub4.boundary: Focuses on model/experiment lifecycle management. Excludes evaluation, benchmarking, and automated workflows.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub4.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily manages ML models or experiments (tracking, registry, execution).

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub5.name: Model Evaluation & Benchmarking
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub5.description: Evaluating, benchmarking, and quality control of AI model outputs using metrics and judges.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub5.boundary: Focuses on output quality assessment. Excludes experiment management, agent testing, and workflow automation.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub5.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily evaluates, benchmarks, or scores AI model output quality.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub6.name: Business Intelligence & Analytics
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub6.description: Analyzing business data, sales metrics, customer information, and financial data for insights.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub6.boundary: Focuses on business operations data. Excludes government data, software logs, and industrial equipment analytics.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub6.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily analyzes business, sales, customer, or financial data for operational insights.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub7.name: Software & System Observability
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub7.description: Analyzing application performance, software logs, traces, telemetry, and system architecture.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub7.boundary: Focuses on software systems and infrastructure. Excludes business data, industrial equipment, and model experiments.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub7.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily analyzes software performance, logs, traces, or system architecture data.

cat_sub24a_sub20_sub9.name: Code Analysis & Execution
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub9.description: Analyzing, generating, refactoring code and executing code in sandboxed environments.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub9.boundary: Covers both static code analysis and dynamic code execution. Excludes software performance monitoring and model evaluation.
cat_sub24a_sub20_sub9.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily analyzes, generates, refactors, or executes source code in isolated environments.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub1.name: General Document Processing
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub1.description: Services for processing, converting, and managing documents in various formats without specialized domain focus.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub1.boundary: Not for domain-specific documents (e.g., code, resumes, ebooks) or specialized analysis (e.g., security, semantic search).
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily handles generic document operations like format conversion, OCR, or basic management across multiple document types, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub2.name: Semantic Search & Retrieval
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub2.description: Services enabling semantic understanding, vector-based search, and retrieval of information from document collections.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub2.boundary: Not for simple keyword search, domain-specific search (e.g., code, tech docs), or document processing without semantic capabilities.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides semantic/vector search, embedding, or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) from documents, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub20.name: Memory & Context Management
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub20.description: Services for persisting, organizing, and retrieving conversational context and memory across sessions.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub20.boundary: Not for document processing, knowledge base management, or real-time search.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub20.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages conversational memory, context persistence, and cross-session recall, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub3.name: Knowledge Management Systems
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub3.description: Services for creating, organizing, and managing personal or organizational knowledge bases and notes.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub3.boundary: Not for general document processing, semantic search engines, or domain-specific knowledge systems (e.g., enterprise knowledge graphs).
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily functions as a knowledge base, note-taking system, or personal knowledge manager, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub4.name: Enterprise Knowledge Systems
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub4.description: Services that structure and provide access to organizational knowledge, often using knowledge graphs.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub4.boundary: Not for personal knowledge management, general semantic search, or public web search.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages and provides access to structured enterprise or project knowledge (e.g., via knowledge graphs), classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub6.name: Web Content & Blog Management
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub6.description: Services for managing, analyzing, and interacting with web content, blogs, and publishing platforms.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub6.boundary: Not for general documents, enterprise systems, or e-commerce platforms.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily connects to or manages web publishing platforms (e.g., WordPress, Substack) and their content, classify here.

cat_sub24a_sub22_sub9.name: Conversation & Chat Analysis
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub9.description: Services for summarizing, analyzing, and extracting knowledge from chat conversations and dialogues.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub9.boundary: Not for document processing, web search, or knowledge base management.
cat_sub24a_sub22_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily processes, summarizes, or extracts knowledge from chat/ conversation transcripts, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub1.name: Code Generation & Scaffolding
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub1.description: Services that automatically generate code, create project structures, or scaffold applications from specifications or configurations.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub1.boundary: Creating new code files, project boilerplate, or application skeletons. Not code execution or general project management.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily creates new code files, project boilerplate, or application skeletons, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub10.name: Project & Task Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub10.description: Services that interact with issue trackers, agile boards, and task management systems to create, query, or update work items.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub10.boundary: Managing tickets, user stories, tasks, or project boards. Not code repository management or CI/CD.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages project tasks or work items, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub11.name: Observability & Feature Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub11.description: Services that collect/analyze telemetry data (logs, metrics) or manage feature flags/experiments for system control.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub11.boundary: Aggregating system health data or toggling features/experiments. Not proactive testing or general configuration.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily monitors system data or manages feature flags/experiments, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub12.name: UI & Application Automation
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub12.description: Services that automate interactions with web browsers, desktop UIs, or mobile applications for testing or workflow automation.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub12.boundary: Controlling browsers, desktop UIs, or mobile apps for automation. Not API testing or game engine automation.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates interactions with UIs or mobile applications, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub13.name: Design & Documentation Integration
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub13.description: Services that bridge design tools with development or generate/analyze technical documentation and knowledge.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub13.boundary: Importing/exporting from design tools or creating/analyzing technical docs. Not general file access or code generation.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily works with design files or technical documentation, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub14.name: AI Agent & Workflow Platform
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub14.description: Services that provide platforms or frameworks specifically for building, orchestrating, or evaluating AI agents and workflows.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub14.boundary: Platforms/frameworks for developing or managing AI agents/workflows. Not using AI within another service or general code execution.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub14.decision_rule: If the service is a platform/framework for developing AI agents or workflows, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub15.name: SDK & API Tooling
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub15.description: Services that provide tooling, servers, or integrations for working with specific SDKs, APIs, or development frameworks.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub15.boundary: Accessing SDK command-line tools, API specifications, code snippets, or framework resources. Not general code generation or execution.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub15.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides tooling for a specific SDK, API, or framework, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub2.name: Code Execution & Sandbox
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub2.description: Services that provide secure environments to execute arbitrary code, manage dependencies, and handle runtime operations.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub2.boundary: Runtime environments for executing user or AI-generated code. Not code generation or testing execution.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a runtime environment for executing code, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub3.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub3.description: Services focused on creating, executing, or analyzing automated tests, including unit, integration, and end-to-end testing.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub3.boundary: Creating, running, or reporting on software tests. Not security scanning or performance monitoring.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily creates, runs, or reports on software tests, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub4.name: Security & Compliance Scanning
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub4.description: Services that scan code, dependencies, or configurations for security vulnerabilities, compliance issues, or sensitive data exposure.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub4.boundary: Identifying security risks, compliance violations, or secrets in code, dependencies, or configs. Not general testing.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily identifies security, compliance, or data exposure risks, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub5.name: Version Control & Repository Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub5.description: Services that interact with Git or other version control systems to manage code repositories, commits, branches, and pushes.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub5.boundary: Operations on version control systems (e.g., Git). Not project management on platforms like GitHub Projects.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs operations on a version control system, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub6.name: Continuous Integration & Delivery
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub6.description: Services that manage CI/CD pipelines, build processes, and deployment automation across various platforms.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub6.boundary: Triggering, managing, or reporting on build and deployment pipelines. Not infrastructure provisioning or post-deployment monitoring.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages build and deployment pipelines, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub7.name: Infrastructure Provisioning & Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub7.description: Services that define, provision, or manage cloud infrastructure through code, templates, or direct API operations.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub7.boundary: Defining infrastructure as code (e.g., Terraform) or performing direct cloud resource operations. Not container orchestration or application deployment.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily provisions or manages cloud infrastructure through code or APIs, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub8.name: Container & Environment Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub8.description: Services that manage containers, development environments, or related configurations for development and runtime.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub8.boundary: Creating, configuring, or managing containerized environments. Not infrastructure provisioning for containers.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages containers or development environments, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub1_sub9.name: Database & Data Management
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub9.description: Services that handle database operations, data pipelines, or data access, including provisioning, queries, and migrations.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub9.boundary: Database lifecycle operations, data pipeline management, or application data access. Not data analysis for analytics.
cat_sub24b_sub1_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily manages databases, data pipelines, or application data access, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub10.name: Cloud Infrastructure & DevOps
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub10.description: Services for managing the lifecycle, configuration, and operations of cloud resources and infrastructure, such as with Alibaba Cloud.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub10.boundary: Not for application-level APIs (like SaaS platforms) or data analysis within cloud services.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub10.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates the provisioning, configuration, or operational management of cloud computing resources and infrastructure, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub11.name: Integration Hubs & Data Pipelines
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub11.description: Services that act as central hubs to connect and move data between a vast number of applications, like Boost.space or the 10k+ tool connector.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub11.boundary: Not for managing a *specific* destination platform (e.g., a CRM) or for building automations *within* a single low-code platform.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily functions as a multi-connector hub for aggregating data from or routing actions to hundreds/thousands of other services, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub12.name: Human-in-the-Loop & Approval Workflows
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub12.description: Services designed to bridge AI/automation and human decision-making by routing tasks for review, input, or approval, like gotoHuman or KirokuForms.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub12.boundary: Not for pure AI-to-AI collaboration or for human-facing productivity tools without a structured review/approval mechanism.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub12.decision_rule: If the service primarily facilitates structured human review, approval, or input within an automated workflow, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub13.name: AI Agent Orchestration & Collaboration
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub13.description: Services providing frameworks for multiple AI agents to discover each other, communicate, and collaborate on complex tasks.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub13.boundary: Not for connecting a single agent to a business tool, or for human-in-the-loop systems.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides the infrastructure for multiple AI agents to register, communicate, and work together, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub15.name: MCP Infrastructure & Governance
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub15.description: Services that provide core infrastructure for the MCP ecosystem, such as gateways, registries, credential management, and routing engines.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub15.boundary: Not for MCP servers that connect to specific business applications (most other categories).
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub15.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides foundational tooling, routing, or governance for the MCP protocol itself, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub20.name: Identity, Security & Trust
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub20.description: Services focused on authentication, authorization, fraud prevention, and establishing trust for AI agents and automated workflows, like the Cheqd service.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub20.boundary: Not for general user management within a specific application (e.g., Salesforce) or for API key management alone.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub20.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides decentralized identity, credential verification, fraud detection, or trust frameworks for autonomous agents, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub18_sub6.name: Low-Code/No-Code Development Platforms
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub6.description: Services for managing and automating workflows within visual development environments like n8n, Make.com, or Simplifier.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub6.boundary: Not for connecting the outputs *of* these platforms to other systems (see Integration Hubs), but for managing the platforms themselves.
cat_sub24b_sub18_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides tools to manage, execute, or build within a low-code/no-code automation or app builder platform, classify here.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub1.name: Data Pipeline & ETL Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub1.description: Services for orchestrating workflows that move, transform, and process data between systems (ELT/ETL).
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub1.boundary: Focus on data extraction, loading, transformation, and pipeline management workflows.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub1.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for data extraction, loading, transformation, or pipeline management. NOT for general task automation or business application workflows.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub10.name: Workflow Platform & API Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub10.description: Services providing APIs or tools to manage and interact with external workflow orchestration platforms.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub10.boundary: Focus on providing programmatic access to manage workflows, executions, and resources on third-party orchestration platforms.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub10.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily provides an API, SDK, or interface to programmatically manage workflows, executions, or resources on an external workflow orchestration platform (e.g., n8n, Conductor). NOT for being the orchestration platform itself.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub2.name: Business Process Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub2.description: Services for orchestrating workflows that integrate and automate core business operations across departments.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub2.boundary: Focus on workflows spanning ERP, project management, productivity suites, and cross-functional business operations.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub2.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for automating processes within ERP systems, managing product/project lifecycles, or coordinating tasks across integrated productivity suites. NOT for specialized technical domains like DevOps or data pipelines.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub3.name: Cloud Infrastructure & DevOps Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub3.description: Services for orchestrating workflows that manage, provision, or operate cloud resources and development operations.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub3.boundary: Focus on cloud resource lifecycle, cluster management, CI/CD, and infrastructure operations.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub3.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for cloud resource lifecycle, cluster management, or CI/CD operations. NOT for data pipelines, business applications, or media management.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub4.name: Knowledge & Content Management Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub4.description: Services for orchestrating workflows for knowledge management, content creation, and learning reinforcement.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub4.boundary: Focus on managing knowledge bases, creating/publishing content, reinforcing learning, and organizing media libraries.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub4.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for managing knowledge bases, creating/publishing content (pages, posts, assets), reinforcing learning, or organizing media libraries. NOT for project management or communication workflows.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub6.name: Multi-Agent & Tool Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub6.description: Services for orchestrating workflows where multiple AI agents coordinate or systems dynamically sequence external tools.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub6.boundary: Focus on coordinating tasks between autonomous AI agents, or dynamically routing to and executing sequences of tools across applications.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub6.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for coordinating tasks between multiple AI agents, or for discovering, routing to, and executing sequences of tools across applications. NOT for single-agent task automation or human-in-the-loop approval workflows.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub7.name: Collaboration & Communication Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub7.description: Services for orchestrating workflows that automate interactions across communication and collaboration platforms.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub7.boundary: Focus on automating SMS, email, chat, virtual meetings, document collaboration, and approval workflows.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub7.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for automating communications (SMS, email, chat), managing virtual meetings, coordinating document collaboration, or managing human review/approval steps. NOT for internal business process automation or media management.

cat_sub24b_sub19_sub9.name: IT, Security & Financial Operations Orchestration
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub9.description: Services for orchestrating workflows for IT incident response, security alerts, and financial/decentralized systems.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub9.boundary: Focus on managing IT incidents, security alerts, on-call schedules, financial trades, blockchain networks, and smart contracts.
cat_sub24b_sub19_sub9.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily orchestrates workflows for managing IT incidents, security alerts, on-call schedules, executing financial trades, or managing blockchain networks/smart contracts. NOT for cloud infrastructure provisioning or software development workflows.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub1.name: General Web Search
cat_sub27_sub2_sub1.description: Services that perform broad, keyword-based searches across the general internet, returning links and summaries from standard search engines.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub1.boundary: Does not include searches within specific platforms (e.g., code, academic), specialized data types (e.g., images, devices), or structured knowledge bases (e.g., wikis).
cat_sub27_sub2_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily performs general-purpose, web-wide keyword searches using mainstream engines (Google, Bing, Brave, etc.), classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub11.name: Aggregated & Meta Search
cat_sub27_sub2_sub11.description: Services that aggregate results from multiple search engines or sources (like SearXNG) to provide consolidated, unbiased results.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub11.boundary: Does not query a single search engine directly or focus on a specific content type. Focuses on result aggregation and redundancy.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily queries and combines results from multiple underlying search engines or public search APIs, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub12.name: AI-Optimized Search
cat_sub27_sub2_sub12.description: Services specifically designed or optimized for use by AI agents and LLMs, often with summarization, citation, or reasoning enhancements.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub12.boundary: Does not include standard search APIs or engines meant for human use. Focuses on features tailored for autonomous AI interaction.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub12.decision_rule: If the service is explicitly marketed for AI/LLM use, offering features like answer synthesis, source citation, or agentic tool-calling, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub13.name: Real-Time & News Search
cat_sub27_sub2_sub13.description: Services that focus on searching for and retrieving real-time information, breaking news, current events, or live data feeds.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub13.boundary: Does not include searching historical data, static knowledge bases, or general web pages without a recency focus.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub13.decision_rule: If the service primarily emphasizes finding the most current information, news articles, or real-time data updates, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub2.name: Structured Knowledge Search
cat_sub27_sub2_sub2.description: Services that search and query structured knowledge bases, encyclopedias, and semantic datasets like Wikidata or Wikipedia.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub2.boundary: Does not include searching the live, unstructured web or general-purpose search engines. Focuses on curated, factual repositories.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily searches or queries structured knowledge graphs, wikis, or curated encyclopedic content, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub20.name: Anti-Blockade Crawling
cat_sub27_sub2_sub20.description: Services specializing in advanced techniques to bypass bot detection, CAPTCHAs, geo-blocks, and other anti-scraping measures on websites.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub20.boundary: Not for general browsing or simple fetching. Distinguished by its core focus on overcoming access restrictions and evasion techniques.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub20.decision_rule: If the service's primary value proposition is reliably accessing content from websites that actively block standard bots/crawlers, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub3.name: Search Engine Optimization
cat_sub27_sub2_sub3.description: Services focused on SEO analysis, keyword research, backlink checking, and SERP data to optimize web content visibility.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub3.boundary: Does not include performing general web searches for information retrieval or analyzing non-SEO-specific website content.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides data or tools for keyword research, ranking analysis, backlink profiling, or other SEO tasks, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub4.name: Website & Page Crawling
cat_sub27_sub2_sub4.description: Services dedicated to programmatically browsing, downloading, and extracting content from specific web pages or entire websites.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub4.boundary: Does not include performing keyword searches across the web or analyzing content for SEO. Focuses on accessing and retrieving page data.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily automates the fetching, rendering, and raw content extraction from given URLs or sitemaps, classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub5.name: Content Format Conversion
cat_sub27_sub2_sub5.description: Services that specialize in converting extracted web content (like HTML) into clean, structured formats such as Markdown or plain text.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub5.boundary: Does not include the initial act of searching for or crawling to find pages. Focuses on post-retrieval content transformation and sanitization.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub5.decision_rule: If the service's core function is to transform messy web page HTML into a clean, usable text format (especially Markdown), classify here.

cat_sub27_sub2_sub9.name: Search Index Management
cat_sub27_sub2_sub9.description: Services that provide interfaces to configure, query, and manage dedicated search indices like Algolia, Elasticsearch, or OpenSearch.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub9.boundary: Does not include crawling the live web or using general search engines. Focuses on interacting with a pre-built, proprietary search index.
cat_sub27_sub2_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily allows querying or administering a custom, hosted search index (e.g., for an app or website), classify here.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub1.name: Software Development & Code
cat_sub27_sub23_sub1.description: Services searching or analyzing source code, APIs, developer documentation, or software repositories.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub1.boundary: Focuses on technical artifacts for software creation. Excludes general web content, business data, or personal documents.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub1.decision_rule: Classify here if the service's primary function is searching/analyzing source code, APIs, dev docs, or software libraries (e.g., GitHub, Stack Overflow).

cat_sub27_sub23_sub11.name: Search Infrastructure & Platforms
cat_sub27_sub23_sub11.description: Services providing underlying search/retrieval platforms (vector DB, search engine) for other apps to build upon.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub11.boundary: Platforms and infrastructure, not end-user applications. Excludes apps searching specific domains for end-users.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub11.decision_rule: Classify here if the service is a general-purpose search engine, vector database, or retrieval platform offered as a service for developers.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub12.name: Hardware & Niche Systems
cat_sub27_sub23_sub12.description: Services searching, controlling, or interacting with specialized hardware, legacy systems, or niche platforms.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub12.boundary: Focuses on specialized hardware control, legacy systems, or niche computing platforms. Excludes general software or web services.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub12.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily interfaces with specialized hardware, legacy computing systems, or niche technical platforms.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub2.name: Web & Public Content
cat_sub27_sub23_sub2.description: Services performing broad web searches, crawling websites, or extracting general public online content.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub2.boundary: Focuses on publicly accessible web content and specialized public data sources. Excludes searches targeting internal corporate data.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub2.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches, crawls, or summarizes general web pages, public websites, or broad online content.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub3.name: Business, Finance & Enterprise
cat_sub27_sub23_sub3.description: Services searching internal business systems, financial data, market news, or enterprise platforms.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub3.boundary: Focuses on corporate operational data, financial instruments, and market information. Excludes public web searches or personal files.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub3.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches internal corporate knowledge, financial data, market news, or enterprise SaaS platforms.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub5.name: Personal & Local Files
cat_sub27_sub23_sub5.description: Services searching, indexing, or interacting with a user's personal documents or private message histories.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub5.boundary: Focuses on an individual's private, locally stored data. Excludes public web content, code, or shared enterprise systems.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub5.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches a user's personal documents, local file systems, or private message databases.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub7.name: Academic & Published Content
cat_sub27_sub23_sub7.description: Services searching, analyzing, or retrieving published content like news, blogs, research papers, or books.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub7.boundary: Focuses on published content for consumption, including scientific literature. Excludes code, financial data, or personal files.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub7.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches news, blogs, newsletters, books, research papers, or entertainment media.

cat_sub27_sub23_sub8.name: AI Tools & Agent Discovery
cat_sub27_sub23_sub8.description: Services searching, indexing, or discovering other AI models, agents, or AI-powered tools.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub8.boundary: Focuses on discovering AI/LLM-based tools within an ecosystem. Excludes searching general web content or domain-specific data.
cat_sub27_sub23_sub8.decision_rule: Classify here if the service primarily searches directories of AI agents, MCP servers, LLM tools, or AI model marketplaces.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub1.name: Productivity & Collaboration
cat_sub30_sub20_sub1.description: Services connecting to productivity suites, project management, and team collaboration tools.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub1.boundary: Tools for team coordination, task management, and collaborative workspaces. Excludes communication-only tools.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub1.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to tools for team productivity, project tracking, or collaborative document editing.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub10.name: AI Model Platforms
cat_sub30_sub20_sub10.description: Services connecting to specific AI models, LLMs, and machine learning APIs.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub10.boundary: Dedicated AI/ML model APIs and inference endpoints. Excludes AI-powered applications.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub10.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to a specific AI model API or ML inference service.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub11.name: AI-Powered Applications
cat_sub30_sub20_sub11.description: Services connecting to AI-enhanced tools for coding, content creation, and analysis.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub11.boundary: Applications that use AI features (coding assistants, content generators). Excludes pure model APIs.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub11.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to applications with built-in AI capabilities.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub12.name: Automation & Workflow
cat_sub30_sub20_sub12.description: Services connecting to workflow automation, business process management, and RPA platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub12.boundary: Platforms for designing, orchestrating, and executing multi-step automated workflows.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub12.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for creating automated workflows or processes.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub13.name: API Integration Platforms
cat_sub30_sub20_sub13.description: Services that connect to multiple APIs or provide API integration capabilities.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub13.boundary: Platforms for managing, connecting, or orchestrating multiple API integrations.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub13.decision_rule: Classify here if service provides multi-API connectivity, API management, or integration orchestration.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub14.name: Business Operations
cat_sub30_sub20_sub14.description: Services connecting to HR, marketing, e-commerce, and business management platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub14.boundary: Platforms for business functions like HR, marketing, retail, and operations management.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub14.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for business operations, HR, marketing, or e-commerce.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub16.name: IT & Service Management
cat_sub30_sub20_sub16.description: Services connecting to IT service management, helpdesk, and IT operations platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub16.boundary: Platforms for IT ticketing, service catalogs, or IT asset management.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub16.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for IT service management or helpdesk operations.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub2.name: Communication
cat_sub30_sub20_sub2.description: Services connecting to email, chat, video conferencing, and messaging platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub2.boundary: Platforms for sending/receiving messages, emails, or conducting meetings. Excludes team collaboration suites.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub2.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for direct communication (email, chat, video).

cat_sub30_sub20_sub3.name: Development & DevOps
cat_sub30_sub20_sub3.description: Services connecting to code repositories, development platforms, CI/CD, and DevOps tools.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub3.boundary: Platforms for software development, version control, issue tracking, or application deployment.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub3.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for coding, testing, or deploying software.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub5.name: Cloud Storage & Infrastructure
cat_sub30_sub20_sub5.description: Services connecting to cloud storage, compute, and infrastructure management platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub5.boundary: Platforms for cloud resource provisioning, object storage, or server management.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub5.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for managing cloud infrastructure or storage services.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub6.name: Data & Analytics
cat_sub30_sub20_sub6.description: Services connecting to databases, data warehouses, BI, and analytics platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub6.boundary: Platforms for querying, analyzing, or visualizing structured business data.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub6.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence.

cat_sub30_sub20_sub9.name: Content Management
cat_sub30_sub20_sub9.description: Services connecting to content management systems, blogging, and publishing platforms.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub9.boundary: Platforms for creating, managing, and publishing digital content.
cat_sub30_sub20_sub9.decision_rule: Classify here if service primarily connects to platforms for content creation, management, or publishing.

_sub30_sub21_sub4.name: Business Operations & Marketing
_sub30_sub21_sub4.description: Services that integrate AI with ERP, CRM, marketing platforms, and core business process systems.
_sub30_sub21_sub4.boundary: Covers enterprise business systems and marketing/campaign management. Excludes general data platforms, financial trading, or content creation for non-business purposes.
_sub30_sub21_sub4.decision_rule: If the service primarily connects AI to systems managing business entities like customers, sales, finances, supply chain, or marketing campaigns/analytics, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub1.name: Software Development & Design
cat_sub30_sub21_sub1.description: Services that assist with coding, codebase analysis, developer tools, software project management, and design workflows.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub1.boundary: Covers software development lifecycle and digital design creation. Excludes general data analysis, business operations, or content generation unrelated to software/design.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily helps write, analyze, manage, or understand software code, development workflows, or visual design files/diagrams, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub11.name: General AI Services
cat_sub30_sub21_sub11.description: Services that provide simplified access to core AI capabilities through APIs or standardized interfaces.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub11.boundary: Covers general-purpose AI model access and simplified AI service interfaces. Excludes domain-specific integrations, model orchestration, or MCP ecosystem tools.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub11.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides simplified API access to core AI/ML capabilities without domain-specific integration, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub16.name: MCP Ecosystem Tools
cat_sub30_sub21_sub16.description: Services that manage, discover, or facilitate use of other MCP servers and AI agents within the MCP protocol.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub16.boundary: Covers MCP server registries, gateways, managers, and marketplaces. Excludes services providing end-user functionality in specific domains.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub16.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides a registry, gateway, manager, or marketplace for MCP servers themselves, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub17.name: AI Model Orchestration
cat_sub30_sub21_sub17.description: Services that enable AI to call, compare, or manage multiple other AI models and machine learning workflows.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub17.boundary: Covers coordination and management of multiple AI/ML models. Excludes using a single AI model as a tool, or data analysis without model selection.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub17.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables an AI to coordinate, evaluate, or call upon multiple other AI/ML models, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub18.name: Database & API Integration
cat_sub30_sub21_sub18.description: Services that provide AI with direct, standardized access to query databases or call generic APIs and microservices.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub18.boundary: Covers raw database queries and arbitrary API calls. Excludes domain-specific platform integrations (Salesforce, Shopify) or search engines.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub18.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides AI with tools to execute raw queries on databases or call arbitrary external APIs/endpoints, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub2.name: Data & Analytics Platforms
cat_sub30_sub21_sub2.description: Services that connect AI to data warehouses, analytics engines, BI platforms, and search interfaces for querying and analysis.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub2.boundary: Covers data analysis, business intelligence, and information discovery across unified sources. Excludes raw data storage without analysis, specialized domains like finance, or single dedicated knowledge bases.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub2.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables AI to query, analyze, visualize data within analytical platforms, or search across multiple sources/unified indices, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub20.name: Real-time Data & Events
cat_sub30_sub21_sub20.description: Services that connect AI to live data streams, event feeds, news, and real-time monitoring systems.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub20.boundary: Covers live, streaming, and real-time updated data. Excludes querying historical databases, static knowledge bases, or batched analytics.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub20.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides AI with access to live, streaming, or real-time updated data feeds and events, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub3.name: Cloud, Infrastructure & Security
cat_sub30_sub21_sub3.description: Services that allow AI to manage cloud resources, servers, core infrastructure, and security/identity functions.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub3.boundary: Covers cloud computing, infrastructure operations, and security/identity management. Excludes application-specific data (CRM, ERP) or end-user productivity tools.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub3.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables AI to provision, configure, or operate cloud compute/storage/networking resources, or verify identities/detect fraud/manage security, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub5.name: Finance & Trading
cat_sub30_sub21_sub5.description: Services that provide AI access to financial markets, trading platforms, cryptocurrency exchanges, and trading execution.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub5.boundary: Covers financial markets, trading, and cryptocurrency execution. Excludes general business data analytics, personal finance, or blockchain data for non-financial use.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub5.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables AI to execute trades, analyze markets, or interact with financial exchanges/cryptocurrencies, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub6.name: Blockchain & Web3
cat_sub30_sub21_sub6.description: Services that enable AI to read blockchain data, execute smart contracts, and interact with decentralized applications.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub6.boundary: Covers blockchain networks, on-chain data, and smart contract functionality. Excludes cryptocurrency trading/finance as primary use case, or general database access.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub6.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides AI with access to blockchain networks, on-chain data, or smart contract functionality, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub7.name: Knowledge & Content Management
cat_sub30_sub21_sub7.description: Services that help AI store, retrieve, reason over organizational knowledge, documents, and web content.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub7.boundary: Covers internal knowledge bases, document corpora, and web content management. Excludes public web search, real-time data feeds, or unstructured personal file storage.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub7.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables AI to search, summarize, answer questions from curated knowledge bases, or manage/publish web content, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub8.name: Multimedia & Entertainment
cat_sub30_sub21_sub8.description: Services that provide AI with tools to analyze, generate, or edit images, audio, video, and interactive media.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub8.boundary: Covers multimedia processing and entertainment/gaming content. Excludes text-only document processing, computer vision for automation, or design tool integration.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub8.decision_rule: If the service primarily provides AI with capabilities to process/generate image/audio/video files or interact with game platforms/entertainment content, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub21_sub9.name: Productivity & Communication
cat_sub30_sub21_sub9.description: Services that enable AI to automate desktop tasks, manage workflows, and integrate with communication/collaboration tools.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub9.boundary: Covers cross-application automation, workflow management, and team communication platforms. Excludes domain-specific automation (trading, cloud ops) or standalone telephony services.
cat_sub30_sub21_sub9.decision_rule: If the service primarily enables AI to automate cross-application tasks, manage workflows, or integrate with platforms for team communication/collaboration, classify here.

cat_sub30_sub3_sub1.name: Task & Project Management
cat_sub30_sub3_sub1.description: Services for managing tasks, to-do lists, projects, and workflows within dedicated task management platforms.
cat_sub30_sub3_sub1.boundary: Not for general note-taking, document collaboration, or calendar scheduling. See sibling categories for those functions.
cat_sub30_sub3_sub1.decision_rule: If the service primarily integrates with or manages a dedicated task/project management tool (e.g., Things3, TickTick, Monday.com), classify here.
